title: magnolia campbellii subsp. mollicomata
intro: Magnolia campbellii subsp. mollicomata, also known as the Yulan Magnolia, is a subspecies of the magnolia plant native to the mountainous regions of China. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its alkaloid and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Yulan Magnolia include reducing fever, relieving cough and sore throat, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and pneumonia. In herbal preparations, the bark and flowers of the plant are typically used to make teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are used to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
